Volume Surge Optimization surge_mult 1.5 → 1.2

BRK+MTF+VOL Tiered T1+T2 | RR 5.0 | Smart DL $1,100 | 10-year backtest (Jan 2016 – Dec 2025) | 699,273 bars
Change: Volume surge threshold from x1.5 to x1.2 — unlocks +1,807 trades with better performance across all metrics

The Change

x1.5 → x1.2
Volume Surge Multiplier (vol_ratio ≥ threshold to confirm breakout)
What is surge_mult? — The vol_surge filter requires current bar volume to be ≥ N× the 20-bar average before any breakout (key level) is recognized. At x1.5, only 15.8% of bars pass. At x1.2, 27.0% pass — unlocking breakouts that were previously blocked despite valid price action.

Impact Summary

Improvements

  • P&L increases by +$432K (+26%) over 10 years
  • Profit Factor improves from 1.52 to 1.57
  • Win Rate rises from 35.9% to 37.3%
  • Max Drawdown decreases from -5.9% to -4.5%
  • P&L/DD ratio jumps from 42.6 to 54.7
  • 126 more successful runner trades (+$338K)
  • Expectancy increases from $165 to $177 per trade
  • BUY win rate improves from 40.2% to 42.0%
  • New signals are same quality as existing (not noise)

Considerations

  • Avg winner slightly lower ($1,321 vs $1,358): more small wins
  • Avg loser nearly identical ($-505 vs $-502)
  • Max consecutive losses: 29 vs 26 (3 more)
  • 1,807 more trades = more commission costs in live trading
  • Validated on 10 years only; real-time monitoring recommended

Why x1.2 Outperforms x1.5

Key Insight: The x1.5 surge filter was too aggressive — it blocked 84% of bars from triggering breakouts. Many of those blocked bars were legitimate breakouts in trending markets. At x1.2, we still filter out low-volume noise but allow breakouts with moderate volume confirmation (+27% of bars pass vs 16%).

Volume Surge Distribution

Threshold% Bars PassingBars with BRK>0Interpretation
x1.5 (current)15.8%~5,500Very strict — misses many valid breakouts
x1.322.4%~7,200Moderate
x1.2 (proposed)27.0%~8,600Balanced — filters noise, catches trends
x1.0 (no filter)39.7%~12,000Too loose — noise starts to dominate

Impact on BRK Engine (Weight = 4/8 for BUY)

The BRK engine has the highest weight for buy signals: BUY_WEIGHTS = [MTF=3, VOL=1, BRK=4].
When surge blocks a breakout, BRK=0, and the maximum possible buy score becomes:
(MTF×3 + VOL×1 + 0×4) / 8 = 5.0 — barely at threshold, needs perfect MTF+VOL to even trigger.

With x1.2, more breakouts register → BRK contributes meaningfully → more quality signals fire.

Where the Extra Profit Comes From

SourceExtra TradesExtra P&L% of Total Gain
TP+Trail (runners)+126+$338,21578%
Timeout (profitable)+774+$495,420115%
TP (full close)+26+$83,22219%
SL (new losers)+874-$498,733-115%
Net+1,807+$432,466100%
The math is clear: The 126 extra runners generate +$338K. The 774 extra timeouts (80.7% win rate) add +$495K. Even after subtracting $499K from 874 new SL hits, the net is +$432K positive. The new signals have the same quality as existing ones — they were just hidden behind an overly strict volume filter.

Statistical Validation

Sample Size
11,687 trades
Highly significant
Test Period

Market Conditions

2016-2025 covers all

Consistency Across Timeframes

Periodx1.5 P&Lx1.2 P&Lx1.2 PFx1.2 Better?
10 years (2016-2025) +$1,649,371 +$2,081,837 1.57 YES (+26%)
2 years (2024-2025) +$468,216 +$595,781 1.74 YES (+27%)
Last 60 days +$22,863 +$4,302 1.07 NO (small sample, ATR outlier)

Why the Last 60 Days Diverged

The 60-day window (Dec 2025 – Mar 2026) was an outlier period:
  • Only 85 trades — statistically insignificant (vs 11,687 over 10 years)
  • ATR of 24.2 was 38% above the 10-year average
  • Only 38% T1 trades vs historical 85% — runners (the profit engine) were rare
  • Choppy sideways market: -869 pts net, 30 up / 29 down days
  • Win rate collapsed to 23.5% vs historical 37.3%

Conclusion: Drawing conclusions from 85 trades in an abnormal market regime would be a classic small-sample fallacy. The 10-year data (11,687 trades) provides the reliable answer.

ParameterValue
EnginesMTF Trend + Volume + Key Levels (no VWAP)

BUY WeightsMTF=3, VOL=1, BRK=4 (total=8)

Trading Hours07:00-23:59 NY
RR Ratio1:5.0
Tier 1 (SL≤25)2 contracts (1 TP + 1 Runner)
